name: thinking-machine-skills
description: A model-agnostic skill pack distilled from public Thinking Machines Lab themes for building collaborative, customizable, reproducible, and empirically grounded AI systems.

resources.name: Experiment Rigor
resources.description: Use when planning, running, reviewing, or writing up ML experiments with strong baselines, ablations, hyperparameter sweeps, raw examples, clean charts, and transparent assumptions.

resources.name: Forecasting Systems
resources.description: Use when building LLM systems for judgmental forecasting, probability prediction, world-event analysis, proper scoring, forecasting-specific fine-tuning, or ensemble forecasts.

resources.name: Interaction Models
resources.description: Use when designing real-time human-AI collaboration, live voice/video/text interfaces, interruption handling, micro-turn interaction, concurrent tool use, visual proactivity, or foreground/background model coordination.

resources.name: LoRA Adapter Training
resources.description: Use when choosing LoRA, adapters, PEFT settings, target modules, rank, learning rate, batch size, or baseline comparisons for efficient LLM post-training.

resources.name: Modular Optimization
resources.description: Use when exploring optimizer and architecture co-design, manifold constraints, tensor health, Stiefel constraints, modular manifolds, Lipschitz-aware learning-rate budgets, or principled neural network training research.

resources.name: Multimodal Customization
resources.description: Use when customizing or fine-tuning models for image, audio, video, screenshot, diagram, classification, or mixed-modality tasks, especially with low data or VLM adapters.

resources.name: On-Policy Distillation
resources.description: Use when training a student model with its own sampled rollouts and dense teacher feedback for reasoning, personalization, behavior recovery, continual learning, or context distillation.

resources.name: Reproducible Inference
resources.description: Use when diagnosing nondeterministic LLM outputs, designing reproducible inference, aligning trainer and sampler numerics, or evaluating batch-size and load-related output drift.

resources.name: Thinking Machine Research Principles
resources.description: Use when shaping an LLM product, research plan, or agent behavior around Thinking Machines-style principles: shared science, customization, human-AI collaboration, solid infrastructure, multimodality, product-research co-design, empirical safety, and real-world value measurement.

resources.name: Training API Abstraction
resources.description: Use when designing a model-agnostic training API, fine-tuning loop, RL loop, distillation backend, checkpoint workflow, or LLM training abstraction inspired by Tinker-style primitives.
